title: Slow Cooker Rice Pudding

overview.description: A creamy traditional British rice pudding slowly cooked until the rice is tender and the milk has thickened naturally. Perfect for batch cooking and freezing.

method: Lightly butter the inside of the slow cooker.
Add the pudding rice, milk, sugar, butter and vanilla.
Sprinkle the nutmeg over the surface.
Stir after the first hour, then once or twice more if convenient.
Continue cooking until the rice is tender and the pudding is thick and creamy.
Serve warm or allow to cool before portioning for freezing.

learning.did-you-know: Rice pudding has been eaten in Britain for centuries and was traditionally baked slowly in the cooling oven after bread had finished baking.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How slow cooking naturally thickens milk and why pudding rice produces a creamier texture than ordinary rice.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A classic comfort pudding that costs very little to make, freezes beautifully and is ideal for keeping homemade desserts ready in the freezer.
chapman-tips: Whole milk gives the creamiest texture without needing cream.
Don't skip the first stir—it prevents the rice settling.
The pudding thickens considerably as it cools.
Leave it slightly looser than you'd like before cooling.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze in individual portions for up to three months.
notes.leftovers: Reheat gently in the microwave or saucepan with a splash of milk.
